# Commands

Commands are the user-facing slash actions exposed by the CLI.

## Registry Model

`commands.ts` imports the command definitions and composes them into a memoized list. Some commands are always available; others are gated by feature flags or runtime conditions.

This registry is intentionally centralized so the CLI can:
- list available commands
- hide internal-only commands from external builds
- filter commands in remote or restricted modes

## Command Shape

Command modules usually follow a common pattern:
- an `index.ts` or `.ts` file that exports the command definition
- a command-specific implementation file or React screen
- command metadata describing the prompt or action

## Relationship to Input Processing

Slash commands are interpreted during user input processing. That means command parsing happens before query execution, which keeps command invocations out of the normal chat flow unless the command explicitly yields a model turn.

## Feature-Gated Commands

The command surface is not static. It changes with build-time features such as bridge mode, coordinator mode, voice mode, workflows, and other internal flags.

## Key Files

- [commands.ts](../../../coding%20agent/commands.ts)
